item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded: After discussing it with the author, I've determined this is sheer cooincidence - and the images are dissimilar enough for me to restore it as CC.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This image is a remixed image derived from the Black Hand logo from Just Cause 1. As such, it is a violation of copyrighted material and has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Been meaning to polish it for a while. Thanks for the reminder!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i want to say this is evil alt foundation, but then i realised this is probably the exact thing the real foundation would do if they found someone spying on one of their cameras

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if I'm crazy, because nothing seems to be bothering anyone else in the comments and its been a couple of years now, but the writing of the initial portion of this article seems really wonky to me, to the point that it was a little confusing. However, once you hit the documents, it reads just fine, and I like those a lot. I downvoted this after the first few paragraphs, but I'm going back to no vote. If the author gives it a good editing pass (after presumably a couple years of practice), I think it'll be one of the better articles in these late 1000s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this article. I read this comment. I read the second Iris. And now I can't break the image of every Foundation in every universe having at least one Iris in captivity, Bioshock Infinite style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think the containment is adequate, as discussed in chat, but I was willing to overlook that if I liked the rest of the article.

I didn't, really. It was okay, but didn't really grab me, and felt far too long. Also the science feels juuuust a little bit too unrealistic to me, and I don't feel like it was explained quite well enough. And I also don't care that much for the core concept of 'woman with mysterious AOE kill powers and also she can communicate through TV signal sent along electric cabling'.

edit: the changed containment procedures are much better. And after reading through it all again, I realised I did actually like this. Vote changed as appropriate!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first one, 105.

I like 1465, but it never has trickled down into the sub-basements of my mind where ideas tend to cross-breed of their own accord like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which Iris?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good, very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My brain has decided that -2 is the alternate universe version of Iris. Due to differences in random quantum fluctuations (which skips are notoriously sensitive to) alt-Iris' power manifested around video instead of still photos. When alt-Foundation initially contained her nobody was aware that she could also influence video in data stream form; she didn't even know about it herself until the experiments in the logs. Now they know, and have modified containment accordingly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How is this concept not already a thing?
It's a wonderful idea and you should write it immediately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…While any circuit with a power supply outside of the anomaly remains unaffected, retaining unchanged voltage, current and resistance outside of the anomalous area…

"Outside the anomol(y/ous area)" doesn't need to be stated twice in the same sentence.

Other than that…moar plz. Want to know moar.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or maybe both. Food for thought~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Incarcerators"? Otherwise, this is even more terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or maybe they're Chaos Insurgency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

The description was dry to me but the logs more than made up for it. I came here hoping for a spoiler but… no luck. Right now I'm between two theories right now: that this is an alternate universe Foundation, or that this is simply another branch/location of the Foundation and the whole damn thing is so big and bureaucratic that they don't even know it. For theory two, the symbol is just there created by a random security guy to throw whoever is watching off the trail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think "captors" works well. The only thing you might consider changing it to is "incinerators," as we're not 100% sure these people "captured" her or are just holding her (Maybe they created her? Maybe they're just jailers.) Whatever you use, it's going to have a negative emotional response. What's important to remember is that the Foundation will still use this language, they just won't have an emotional response to it themselves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a real pleasure to read. I only feel some sympathy for the woman because I wonder how she's being treated, or if she's being used for some evil purpose. Freaks me out whenever organizations other than the Foundation are in control over something. I have no problem with them being described as her "captors" because even if they are doing the exact same thing as the Foundation, as far as anyone knows they are not a part of the same entity. Plus after causing the deaths of Foundation personnel they definitely aren't going to be viewed as anything more than a threat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Obligatory mention that Accelerando appears to have typed [DATA REDACTED] incorrectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Captors was a word suggested to me in the absence of other, rather failed attempts not to give these people any presumed names or titles. It wasn't really an attempt to draw sympathies, just a matter of poor wording, I guess. If you've got a better suggestion I'd gladly change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted this for two reasons—one, I guess you're kind of unlucky, because this was the straw that broke the camel's back for me in terms of alt-Foundations. Like, yet another…

Two, I'm not sure I really agree with the Foundation's stance towards SCP-1945-2. Her captors? I can feel you trying to draw my sympathies towards her, and….I don't know. It seems kind of OOC. The Foundation is portrayed here as some sort of ally to SCP-1945-2, which I don't think jives with the Foundation…I guess thematically. These people are not liberators, they are captors and jailers. And I'd expect that they'd view the alternate version of their captors…somewhat more sympathetically. "An attack on the Foundation"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I opened the last collapsible and saw the image I thought "shit, they inserted a MKA in the article", shaked my head, and kept reading. While it's something silly, it means you got me inside of the story. This is totally a great hook. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nothing better than a long article that doesn't show its length. It just keeps me wanting to read more and more.

Also this puts the kibosh on an idea that I had that wasn't nearly as good. Nice to finally get that one off my chest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [DATA REDACTED] is acceptable form.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really good first article. Puts my own firstie to shame. Well done! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was enjoyable. You might want to replace that [DATA REFACTED] with expunged, and there are some periods missing here and there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great article. Creeps abound, minor pity of the SCP-subject before it's drug off by the eerie alt-foundation. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, alright, I think I see the distinction better now. Would it be more accurate to say that a meme deals with abstracted/conceptual/cultural information, whereas a cognito-hazard deals with _any_ kind of sensory information? IE, you have to understand a Meme, up you only have to perceive a cognitohazard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a piece of information that has an effect on the human brain, so it's memetic. The difference between a cognitohazard and a meme is that a cognitohazard is not necessarily information (it could be a chemical or a physical object), while a meme isn't necessarily even anomalous (see: any doge).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The kill agent described here is infectious—the idea was put into a TV broadcast and sent to us, and one of our researchers was "infected" with the idea by watching it. It's the same principle behind the (admittedly pretty fucking campy) Berryman-Langford memetic kill agent, which I assume was the inspiration. I really liked the idea of continuing that kind of thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's worth noting that memetic and cognitohazard are not mutually exclusive terms, and a cultural-context-dependent reproducible piece of information will still be a meme, even if its effect does not directly cause it to spread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking good shit. I stay around the wiki waiting for articles like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But I think what's described here is just a flat out cognito-hazard.

I think you're right, but "memetic kill agent" is one of those relics of site canon that just… Well, it sounds better than "cognitohazardous kill agent", so it sticks around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You almost lost me with a dry description. I remember reviewing part of this for you and then getting pulled away on baby duties.

…Those logs man. Holy shit. I'm successfully creeped out.

+1. Bravo. You're good at this. Work on your tone a little more and you'll be great at it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good. The only issue I have is the formatting of the last image. It looks kinda eh and breaks up the text. I'd prefer for it to be formated the same way normal scp pics are usually formated, only relative to the last collapsible. Maybe that's just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This… this is good shit. Good pacing, good elision. I want to see more stuff like it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think memetic kill agents are nonsensical in and of themselves (Bascially it would be a propagating meme that eventually kills you). But I think what's described here is just a flat out cognito-hazard. IE you see it, you go nuts, and then you kill everyone in the room plus yourself. To be memetic, I think it has to be infectious in some way. (This is mostly from definitions I'm getting out of the tag guide - it's a pretty nice reference)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Slow to start, and not much of a hook until late in the article. But at the end, this one gets an Upvote simply because. This article is very deserving of a tale now….

edit: This hasn't aged well with me. While I like this concept still, I've noticed one large inconsistency. In the logs, you allude to the fact that audio isn't coming through ("It appears she heard something through the intercom" "It appears she heard something on the other side of the door"), but later you state that the audio dropped "for the first time". This jarred me too much, and I had to rescind my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its posts like this one that makes me wish Wikidot had a 'like' button. Either an -EX or a Tale would be in order on that premise…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this one, but now I have the desire to write an -EX of the foundation classifying and accidentally surveiling a manifestation of another SCP in containment and not realising it due to its own bureaucracy and secrecy requirements. For a while I actually thought that might be the twist of this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Memetic kill agents" are nonsensical in my opinion but the rest of it is just the right kind of indirectly menacing weirdness. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately, no. It would be cool if I was edgy enough to make references but no XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alt-Foundation doesn't like us interfering with their containment efforts.

Edit: Also, is that a reference to sandrewswann's 001 proposal?
